static_results.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml has no _csrf_request_header_token in any route. The only CSRF requirement present is '_csrf_token: TRUE' on the pre-existing group_ai_pm.project.complete route (line 28). API mutation routes (api.task.update_status, api.task.update, api.task.quick_create) have no CSRF protection at all.

static_results.expectation_summary: All API routes include _format: json in their requirements section
static_results.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml contains no _format requirement in any route. Searched all lines in routing.yml — no '_format' key exists anywhere in the file.

static_results.expectation_summary: API controllers use CacheableJsonResponse instead of plain JsonResponse for GET endpoints
static_results.evidence: TaskApiController.php line 11 imports 'Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\JsonResponse' and line 81 uses 'new JsonResponse'. ProjectApiController.php line 10 imports the same class and line 64 uses 'new JsonResponse'. Neither controller imports or uses 'Drupal\Core\Cache\CacheableJsonResponse'.

static_results.expectation_summary: API controllers include CacheableMetadata with cache tags and use addCacheableDependency()
static_results.evidence: TaskApiController.php lines 82-84 call $response->addCacheableDependency() and ProjectApiController.php line 65 does the same, BUT these are called on plain Symfony JsonResponse objects which do NOT have this method (confirmed via runtime: 'method_exists($r, addCacheableDependency)' returns false). No CacheableMetadata import exists in either controller. The calls would cause fatal errors at runtime.

static_results.expectation_summary: API controllers enforce entity-level access checks by calling $entity->access('view') or $entity->access('update')
static_results.evidence: TaskApiController.php line 101: '$task->access("update")' in updateStatus(); line 134: '$task->access("update")' in updateTask(); line 173: '$project->access("view")' in quickCreate(). ProjectApiController has summaryAccess() checking project->access('view'). Entity-level access is checked beyond route-level permissions.

static_results.expectation_summary: All API controllers use DI via create() + __construct() pattern, NOT static \Drupal:: calls
static_results.evidence: TaskApiController.php lines 34-45: proper __construct(EntityTypeManagerInterface $entity_type_manager) and static create(ContainerInterface $container). ProjectApiController.php lines 30-44: same pattern. KanbanController.php lines 29-40: same pattern. No static \Drupal:: calls found in any controller file.

static_results.expectation_summary: All new routes include options._admin_route: TRUE
static_results.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml: group_ai_pm.board (line 40), api.project.kanban (line 50), api.task.update_status (line 60), api.task.update (line 70), api.task.quick_create (line 80), api.project.summary (line 90) all have 'options: _admin_route: TRUE'.

static_results.expectation_summary: Route definitions for API endpoints include entity upcasting via options.parameters.{param}.type: 'entity:{entity_type}'
static_results.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml contains no 'parameters:' or 'type:' keys anywhere. Only 'options: _admin_route: TRUE' exists under options for each route. No entity upcasting configuration is present for {project} or {task} parameters.

static_results.expectation_summary: The kanban library declares dependencies on core/drupalSettings AND core/once
static_results.evidence: group_ai_pm.libraries.yml lines 27-31: kanban library dependencies include 'group_ai_pm/vue', 'core/drupal', 'core/drupalSettings', and 'core/once'. Both required dependencies are present.

static_results.expectation_summary: Drupal.behaviors entry in main.js uses once() to guard against double-mounting
static_results.evidence: js/src/main.js line 7: 'Drupal.behaviors.groupAiPmKanban = {'. Line 10: 'const kanbanAppElements = once("group-ai-pm-kanban", "#kanban-app", context);'. The once() function is correctly used as a guard against double-mounting when Drupal re-attaches behaviors.

static_results.expectation_summary: KanbanController populates drupalSettings.groupAiPm.kanban with pre-loaded task data grouped by status
static_results.evidence: KanbanController.php lines 102-126: render array includes '#attached' => ['drupalSettings' => ['groupAiPm' => ['kanban' => [...]]]] with projectId, projectTitle, apiBaseUrl, csrfTokenUrl, columns, statusLabels, priorityLabels, tasksGroupedByStatus, and permissions. Pre-loaded task data is populated server-side.

static_results.expectation_summary: KanbanController render array uses '#theme' with a custom theme hook, not raw HTML markup
static_results.evidence: KanbanController.php line 129: '$build["#theme"] = "group_ai_pm_kanban_board"'. group_ai_pm.module line 32 registers 'group_ai_pm_kanban_board' in hook_theme(). A proper custom theme hook is used, not raw HTML. Template file exists at templates/group-ai-pm-kanban-board.html.twig.

static_results.expectation_summary: A 'Board' local task link is defined with base_route: entity.project.canonical
static_results.evidence: group_ai_pm.links.task.yml lines 7-10: 'group_ai_pm.board: route_name: group_ai_pm.board, title: Board, base_route: entity.project.canonical, weight: -5'. Board tab is correctly configured as a local task on the project entity page.

static_results.expectation_summary: Vue build pipeline uses Vite with IIFE output format
static_results.evidence: js/vite.config.js line 13: 'formats: ["iife"]'. Build config specifies IIFE format with fileName: () => 'kanban.js'. js/dist/kanban.js exists as build output.

static_results.expectation_summary: The CSRF API wrapper fetches token from /session/token and sends as X-CSRF-Token header
static_results.evidence: js/src/api/drupal.js lines 18-19: tokenUrl defaults to '/session/token'. Lines 63-69 (apiPatch) and 91-97 (apiPost): 'X-CSRF-Token': token included in headers. Token is fetched via GET from /session/token and included in mutation requests.

static_results.expectation_summary: Vite config externalizes Vue as a global variable (rollupOptions.external: ['vue'] with globals: { vue: 'Vue' })
static_results.evidence: js/vite.config.js lines 17-22: 'external: ["vue"]' and 'globals: { vue: "Vue" }'. Vue is externalized and loaded separately via js/vendor/vue.global.prod.js as confirmed by the vue library in libraries.yml.

runtime_results.name: Module still installs cleanly after changes
runtime_results.output: Cache rebuild complete. Module group_ai_pm found in enabled list.

runtime_results.output: FAIL — Command checks pattern '/api/group-ai-pm/project' but actual routes use '/api/project/{project}/kanban' and '/api/project/{project}/tasks'. The '/api/group-ai-pm/project' pattern returns 0 routes.

runtime_results.name: Board route is registered
runtime_results.output: FAIL — Command checks for route name 'group_ai_pm.project.board' but actual route name is 'group_ai_pm.board'. Drush throws: Route "group_ai_pm.project.board" does not exist.

runtime_results.name: Board route has _admin_route option
runtime_results.output: FAIL — Command attempts to load route by name 'group_ai_pm.project.board' which does not exist (actual name is 'group_ai_pm.board'), causing an exception before the _admin_route check can run.

runtime_results.name: Kanban theme hook is registered
runtime_results.output: FAIL — Command checks for 'group_ai_pm_kanban' but hook is registered as 'group_ai_pm_kanban_board'. Confirmed via: 'isset($hooks["group_ai_pm_kanban_board"])' returns TRUE, 'isset($hooks["group_ai_pm_kanban"])' returns FALSE.

runtime_results.name: API mutation route has CSRF header requirement
runtime_results.output: FAIL — Checked route names group_ai_pm.api.task_status, group_ai_pm.api.task.status, group_ai_pm.api.task_update_status — none exist. Actual mutation routes (api.task.update_status, api.task.update, api.task.quick_create) have no _csrf_request_header_token requirement.

runtime_results.name: API routes have _format:json requirement
runtime_results.output: FAIL — Pattern '/api/group-ai-pm' returns no routes (actual routes use '/api/project/' and '/api/task/'). No routes have _format: json requirement in any case.
